methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
DNA Script is a Series C-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies, with particular focus on 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables: Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two triangulation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring on the $200M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applies a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to imply $340M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-stage biotech peers yields a $2.4B implied valuation, with the peer range spanning $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue disclosure for DNA Script, a limitation endemic to private company analysis. The public sector P/S median of 5.8x across the three tickers is therefore not deployable here.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $289M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide spread reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set and the tension between stage-progression mechanics and direct peer positioning. Key uncertainties include near-term revenue trajectory, clinical validation milestones, and the underlying capital efficiency of the peer group.
